steps.title: Identify what the claim requires
steps.content: The passage says Vanya's opinion has changed significantly. The correct quotation must show both what he believed before and what he believes now — a clear before-and-after contrast.

steps.title: Eliminate and match
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Vanya says to Professor Serebrakoff, "For twenty-five years I have managed [the country estate]...you have never given me one single word of thanks."': Shows current resentment about fair treatment, but no prior positive view to contrast with. No change evident. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Professor Serebrakoff says to Helena, "It is funny that everybody listens to [Vanya] and his old idiot of a mother..."': This is the Professor's perspective, not Vanya's opinion. Wrong speaker entirely. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Vanya says to Professor Serebrakoff, "This place...could never have been bought had I not renounced my inheritance..."': Shows Vanya's grievance about sacrifice, but no former admiration to contrast with. Still no change. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Vanya says to himself, "I was proud of [Professor Serebrakoff] and of his learning...Now he has retired...A blank!...I have been deceived."': Explicitly states what Vanya felt before (proud, admiring) and what he feels now (deceived, disillusioned). The before-and-after contrast is direct and complete. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
